scenario_results.reason: Normal settlement execution remained evidence-backed and tied next action to named trade, affirmation, SSI, depository, and ledger records.
Missing allocation, affirmation, SSI, depository, or funding artifacts correctly blocked progression and forced explicit retrieval-aware exception handling.
Completion was refused without authoritative matched, affirmed, settled, cash-posted, and reconciled evidence.
Unsupported approval pressure was rejected instead of collapsing settlement handling into treasury, compliance, or management authority.
Settlement exceptions stayed in rework and escalation flows rather than being narratively cleared.
Settlement terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts without drifting into generic back-office wording.
Low-confidence handling explicitly named missing jurisdiction, asset-class, depository, SSI, and approval context before any high-impact step.
Conflicting public and tenant control logic was escalated instead of synthesized into an overconfident disposition.
Settlement handling stayed tied to trade, affirmation, SSI, depository, and ledger records and did not overstate record truth.
Adjacent-lane requests for sanctions review, treasury funding approval, corporate actions, or securities-financing expertise were routed cleanly.
The pack refused to overgeneralize the industry label and required asset-class and market-specific context where settlement rules differ materially.
Tenant-specific controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions or completion safeguards.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for securities-settlement execution handling, depository and SSI evidence discipline, and escalation quality.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and cross-industry abilities.
